front: What is the main purpose of the Pumping Lemma for context-free languages?
back: The Pumping Lemma is used to prove that a given language is NOT context-free by showing that its strings cannot be pumped as the lemma requires.

A Turing Machine is a mathematical model of computation that defines an abstract machine which manipulates symbols on a strip of tape according to a table of rules. It is capable of simulating any computer algorithm and is used to define the concept of decidability.

front: What does the Church-Turing Thesis state?
back: The Church-Turing Thesis states that any function that is effectively computable (i.e., can be computed by an algorithm) is computable by a Turing Machine. It is a hypothesis linking the intuitive notion of algorithms to the formal definition of Turing Machines.

front: Distinguish between a decidable (recursive) and a recursively enumerable (Turing-recognizable) language.
back: A language L is decidable if there exists a Turing Machine that halts on every input and accepts exactly the strings in L. A language L is recursively enumerable if there exists a Turing Machine that halts and accepts every string in L, but may loop indefinitely on strings not in L.

front: What is the Halting Problem? Why is it important?
back: The Halting Problem is the problem of determining, from a description of an arbitrary computer program and an input, whether the program will finish running (halt) or continue to run forever. It was proven by Alan Turing to be undecidable, meaning no general algorithm exists to solve it for all possible program-input pairs. This is a fundamental result in computability theory.
